episodes.title: I Thought I Was Too Smart for a Cult
episodes.description:
I thought I was too smart to be manipulated by a high-control group. I was wrong. In this video, I explore how modern spirituality's emphasis on authenticity, inner alignment, and personal freedom can sometimes make people easier—not harder—to control. Drawing on my experience in Thelema, I examine cult psychology, spiritual bypassing, incentive structures, and why real freedom depends on more than good intentions.

👉 "AI Without Delusion" (FREE PDF GUIDE)
👉 "Resilience at the Turning of the Sky" (FREE PDF GUIDE)
👉 The Ouroboros System: AI-Powered Self-Development By Design
👉 The First Turn

episodes.title: The Spirituality That Made Abuse Feel Like Growth
episodes.description:
Lon Milo DuQuette is one of the most respected teachers in modern occultism and a senior leader in O.T.O. (Ordo Templi Orientis). In this video, I examine how spiritual ideas can be used to reinterpret conflict, dysfunction, and even harm as opportunities for growth. Drawing on DuQuette's public talks and my own experience inside O.T.O., I explore the relationship between Thelema, spiritual bypassing, agency, and the subtle psychological dynamics that can make high-control groups difficult to recognize from the inside.

The Hermits of O.T.O.

Survival: The First and Last Ordeal of Initiation by Lon DuQuette

episodes.title: I Wasn't Brainwashed. I Was Worse.
episodes.description:
For years, I helped build, defend, and legitimize a spiritual community that I believed was doing good. This video is a confession. I was never deceived. I played a necessary role making harmful ideas and behaviors seem normal, reasonable, and safe.

episodes.title: I Thought Leaving Would Destroy Me
episodes.description:
The moment before leaving a high-control group can feel like you're about to step off a cliff. In this video, I explore what that experience was like for me: the fear, the confusion, and the paralysis.

This is not just a story about cults but about what it's like to free your perception and your identity from entanglement in dysfunction. And why reclaiming your agency often begins with acting while still afraid.

episodes.title: How to know when it's time to go
episodes.description:
How do you know when it’s time to walk away from a system, a community, or a relationship that no longer serves you?

In this video, I share my personal story of leaving a high-control spiritual group and the difficult questions that came with it. We’ll talk about why leaving is so hard, the myths that keep us stuck, and the moment of clarity when the choice becomes unavoidable.

This isn’t just about OTO—or any one group. It’s about the universal experience of realizing: The dream doesn’t match reality. And the cost of staying is yourself.

If you’ve ever felt like a ghost in the room, rehearsed your truth in private but filtered it in public, or asked yourself, “Am I the problem?”—this video is for you.

✨ Topics covered:

Why leaving is harder than people think
The subtle signs you’re approaching the threshold
The inner shift that makes leaving inevitable
Clarifying the real choice: authenticity vs. self-abandonment

🔔 Subscribe for more videos on truth, transformation, and reclaiming your authentic self.

Develop a self free of oppressive systems and relationships.

episodes.title: How to leave without losing yourself
episodes.description:
Leaving a high-control group, family, or spiritual system isn’t just about walking away. It’s about reclaiming your identity.

In this video, I share what it really takes to leave without collapsing: how to recognize the signs, face the fear, and find your way back to yourself. If you’ve ever felt erased by the people around you—or afraid to speak your truth—this is for you.

episodes.description:
They don’t need to ban you to control you—just keep you guessing.

From vanishing emails and weaponized gossip to social media surveillance and quiet threats, I lay out 5 disturbing patterns I witnessed firsthand as a leader inside the Order.

If you’ve ever felt watched, silenced, or gaslit in the name of “spiritual growth,” this video will hit home.

episodes.title: OTO's Governance by Gaslighting
episodes.description:
“It’s not abuse. It’s just… process.”

In this video, I break down how spiritual communities like Ordo Templi Orientis (O.T.O.) don’t just enable abuse through explosive personalities or overt power grabs—but through “reasonable” people who speak in the language of care, growth, and emotional intelligence.

They don’t stop the harm.
They normalize it.
They protect it.
And they gaslight the people who try to name it.

Drawing from over a decade inside O.T.O. as a member and local leader, I expose the subtle psychological patterns that allow a high-control structure to mask itself as a spiritual path. These include:

• Emotional reframing (“You just need to talk it out”)
• Weaponized process and policy
• Pathologizing dissent as ego or trauma
• Silence disguised as compassion
• And the relentless moral pressure to stay “fraternal” while staying silent

If you’ve ever spoken up and been punished for it—if you’ve watched people confuse manipulation with mentorship—this video is for you.

👉 You’re not crazy. You’re not alone. And you’re not the problem.

These are the author’s opinions, offered for education and recovery support, not allegations of illegal conduct.

Develop a self free of oppressive systems and relationships.

episodes.title: Speak Up, Get CRUSHED: How OTO Handles Dissent
episodes.description:
What really happens when you speak up inside OTO?

They say, “Stay and fix it from within.” But when you confront real harm—when you name what others are whispering—you don’t get reform. You get punished.

This video exposes what actually happens to those who try to hold OTO accountable: the retaliation, the gaslighting, the quiet erasure. Based on my 11 years inside the Order, it’s not theory—it’s lived experience.

If you’ve ever spoken up and paid the price, you’re not alone.

Develop a self free of oppressive systems and relationships.

episodes.title: When the leaders in OTO are the most unstable ones
episodes.description:
When I was in OTO, I couldn’t understand why volatility, threats, and even abuse seemed to be tolerated. Again and again, I watched people who I thought had manipulative or violent tendencies be treated with kid gloves—or even promoted.

And every time I raised concerns, I was met with "reasons" that sounded more like excuses.

Eventually, I concluded: this wasn't a bug in the system. It was the system. A culture seemingly built to evade accountability, gaslight dissent, and punish those who speak up.

I couldn't keep welcoming people into something I couldn't protect them from.

So I did what my integrity demanded: I left. And I spoke out.

https://lapis-mercurii.kit.com/transmission

episodes.title: When it's no longer safe to have a self
episodes.description:
This video explores how spiritual communities, institutions, and group cultures can slowly make it unsafe to have a self—not through violence or dogma, but through praise, performance, and the subtle erosion of personal boundaries.

Drawing on my own experience in a high-control environment, I name the patterns that leave people afraid to speak up, afraid to say “no,” and eventually afraid to trust their own clarity.

You don’t need to be in a cult to feel this.

If you’ve ever felt punished for telling the truth…
If you've been praised for your silence but punished for your sovereignty…
If you've had to shrink to stay in the room—

This video is for you.

And if you find yourself standing on the edge, unsure what to do, then you should read The Leaving Rite next: https://lapis-mercurii.kit.com/leaving-rite

episodes.title: What happens when you leave a high-control group
episodes.description:
When someone speaks the truth about a high-control group, the system rarely responds with honesty. Instead, it tries to redefine the person who spoke.

In this video, I share what happened after I left a spiritual organization I served for over a decade—and how predictable patterns of retaliation, distortion, and character assassination began to unfold.

If you’re experiencing disillusionment, retaliation, or subtle pressure to stay silent, this video is for you. You’re not crazy. You’re not alone. And you’re not the problem.

episodes.title: This is what spiritual control looks like
episodes.description:
What if control didn’t look like rage, threats, or overt domination? What if it looked like care?

In this video, I explore the quieter forms of spiritual control—the kind that masquerade as concern, kindness, or "just trying to help." These are the patterns I saw not just in my former spiritual community, but in many systems that claim to be about liberation while subtly enforcing conformity.

This isn’t about one group. It’s about a broader dynamic: how systems maintain control by shaping our sense of self, confusing us about what’s true, and training us to betray our instincts in order to belong.

If you've ever walked away from a place that once felt like home—and questioned your memory, your judgment, or your worth—this is for you.

📌 New to this channel? Start here: https://youtu.be/y0gxccYR-QE

episodes.title: Why I Left OTO —And What I Saw
episodes.description:
After more than a decade in Ordo Templi Orientis, I walked away. Not because I stopped believing—but because staying meant abandoning myself. This is not an exposé. It’s a reckoning: a personal account of clarity, consequence, and the cost of staying silent too long.

Everything I’m sharing is my own perception, based on contemporaneous records and direct participation. I’m happy to correct any verifiable error. If anyone believes I’ve misstated a fact, please reach out; I’m committed to accuracy.

The Leaving Rite—A Field Guide to Leaving a System Without Losing Yourself
https://lapis-mercurii.kit.com/leaving-rite

After this, watch: https://youtu.be/mDNrmGDvToM?si=y9R0z3bLHZ099nVf
